# Compile-verification build — Eliza Voice IME + AOSP glue (#12185 sub 5 + 6)

Command (from packages/app-core/platforms/android, in the isolated worktree):

    ELIZA_ANDROID_SKIP_FORK_LLAMA_LIB=1 \
    JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21/... \
    ./gradlew :app:assembleDebug -PelizaCloudBuild=false

Result: BUILD SUCCESSFUL (720 tasks). Compiled the new
ElizaVoiceInputMethodService.java, the voice-keyboard layout/drawables,
res/xml/method.xml, and the manifest service block cleanly.

Worktree setup (shares parent node_modules, same lane #12291 proved):
  - node_modules symlinked to the parent eliza checkout
    (parent .bun has the exact @capacitor+android@8.3.4+1b808583819c2ac6
    the committed capacitor.settings.gradle references).
  - staged the gitignored app/libs/android-js-engine-release.aar from the
    parent tree (background-runner dependency).

This is a compile-verification APK (no web renderer bundle, no on-device
inference lib), exactly as #12291's sub-4 evidence. It proves the whole IME
native surface compiles, packages, installs, is selectable, renders, and its
real code path fires (loopback ASR probe). The full record -> transcribe ->
commit path needs the on-device engine that ships in `bun run build:android`.
